allocationStatement.reserveCustodyDisclosure: The owner-held reserve is publicly disclosed and separated from the deployer wallet, but it is not currently locked, vested, or multisig-controlled.

intendedUses: future ecosystem operations after public disclosure
future community or member benefit transfers only after manual support review
future liquidity, treasury, contributor, or community transfers only after on-chain traceability is available
reserveUseBoundaries.lpLockNote: No LP lock is claimed in this statement. A future LP lock, if executed, should be documented with exact platform, lock address, transaction hash, unlock date, and custody details.

reviewerSummary.holderConcentrationResponse: The 600,000,000 GCA owner-held reserve is disclosed as a reserve wallet rather than hidden circulation. This disclosure does not remove concentration risk by itself; it makes the reserve wallet, transfer transactions, and custody status explicit for reviewers.
reviewerSummary.circulationCaveat: The public 40/60 split is a target allocation disclosure. Data platforms may still calculate circulating supply differently until they accept reserve treatment and publish their own methodology.
reviewerSummary.currentRiskDisclosure: The reserve remains owner-controlled and can move unless later transferred to a lock, vesting contract, or published multisig. Public materials must not describe the reserve as locked.

safeClaims: GCA has a publicly disclosed owner-held reserve wallet.
Two on-chain transfers moved 600,000,000 GCA to the disclosed reserve wallet.
The reserve wallet is separate from the deployer wallet.
The reserve is owner-controlled and not currently locked, vested, or multisig-controlled.
No LP lock is currently claimed.
doNotClaim: reserve lock before on-chain custody changes
LP lock before a verifiable LP lock is executed
burned supply before a burn transaction exists
external audit approval before an independent report is published
deep liquidity, price support, or return promises
